title: Foresty Garden
description: The Foresty Garden Residence is a modern, resort-inspired sanctuary. On a compact site of 120 sqm, we maximize space with a multi-level design blending light-colored wood, stone, and expansive frameless glass, creating a seamless indoor-outdoor connection. Inspired by an Ubud retreat, the interior uses extensive wood on the floors, walls, and ceiling for a cozy treehouse studio. This is mixed with the natural look of concrete and stone textures, creating a home that is simple, luxurious, and peaceful for the family.

title: Edge
description: Designed as a breathable home for a family of five, the Edge House creates a welcoming connection to its neighborhood through a unique semi-public front plaza. This terrace celebrates the homeowner's weekend hobby of bike riding. A roster brick garage opens to park bikes after a ride, allowing fellow bikers to gather and relax in the plaza. The home's facade makes a modern and natural statement with a black and white color palette complemented by a wood louvre second skin. This dynamic facade connects the inside to the outdoors and projects an interesting play of light and shadow throughout the interior. Within the inside space, a large void and a functional attic provide a surprising sense of spaciousness. It crafts a comfortable environment perfect for family life.

title: Spanish Villa
description: The Spanish Villa is an architecture and interior project located in Villa Cinere Mas, designed as a dreamy, seaside revival for a family of four. Inspired by Andalusia, the home creates a relaxing atmosphere with an emphasis on flow throughout the space. The design features classic arches, a welcoming foyer and a fountain. This vision is realized through a rich palette of materials including terracotta, stone, detailed woodwork and vibrant patterns.

title: Halaman
description: Rumah Halaman in Kuningan, Jawa Barat, is a unique home designed for two young families on a single 161 sqm site. Its most innovative feature is a central, semi-public hallway finished with local terracotta, which acts as a connecting bridge and playground where the families can interact. Inspired by Scandinavian design, the home uses a large skylight to create an open and breezy atmosphere. The vision of communal living is completed by a shared backyard and garden, allowing both families to enjoy time together.

title: Cempaka
description: For a family of four in Rempoa, Rumah Cempaka was redesigned from an enclosed building into an open, village-style home that embraces its community. A key feature of this architectural renovation was the creation of a public path through the property, physically connecting the home to its neighborhood. The design showcases a vernacular Javanese style through exposed red brick, concrete, and the reuse of local materials. Inside, an open-plan layout combines vintage and eclectic elements with inherited furniture, crafting a truly homey atmosphere.

title: Grey
description:
Blending Japanese and Scandinavian design, the Grey House in Kalimalang is a simple and modern home created for a young family. Built on a compact 98 sqm site, the design uses a distinctive concrete roster facade to create a fresh, urban look while maximizing natural light and airflow.

The interior is open-plan, combining a soft palette of grey, white, and wood to create a relaxing atmosphere. With a focus on easy maintenance and functional spaces, this home is perfectly suited for a flexible city lifestyle.

client: Family of three and their extended family
description: On a narrow site that only has 4.85 m width, in a dense Mampang neighborhood, this home was designed to cleverly house an extended family. Using a split-level layout, the design remarkably fits seven compact bedrooms and two kitchens. The living spaces are both smart and social; a long wooden dining table doubles as a meeting area, while the upstairs living room features unique, built-in amphitheater seating. The home feels open and airy thanks to excellent cross-ventilation and skylights, and a large front garden provides a rare patch of green in the urban environment.

title: DGP
description: The DGP Residence, located in Duta Gema Pesona Depok, is a large, modern luxury home designed as a serene, resort-like sanctuary for a big family's parents. While the home has a modern feel, the design intentionally incorporates local elements to create a more personal atmosphere. This approach includes using local materials like concrete roster and integrating handcrafted, patterned metal louvres on the facade. Situated on a spacious 450 sqm suburban site, these local touches complement the home's wide views and strong connection to its outdoor lawn, creating a private and welcoming environment for the family to gather.
